title: Anchor All-Hands Q1 2026
compiled_truth:
The first company-wide all-hands for [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) took place on April 1st, 2026, with all four team members present. [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) opened the meeting with a candid state-of-the-company address, walking through burn rate, current runway, and the overall thesis that deterministic data infrastructure still represents a massivley underserved market. Seren emphasized that Q1 had been primarily about proving out the single-pass primitives in controlled environments and that Q2 needed to shift toward external validation — getting real bytes flowing through real systems with real partners.

[Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) presented the engineering OKR review. Of the five key results set in January, three were hit: the core pipeline achieved sub-millisecond latency on the reference benchmark, the integration test suite reached 94% coverage (target was 90%), and the internal dogfooding environment was stood up on schedule. The two misses were around documentation (still patchy) and the SDK packaging work, which got deprioritized when Dani had to chase down a subtle nondeterminism bug in the batching layer that ate almost three weeks. Dani was blunt about this — "we need to stop treating docs as something we'll get to later." Seren agreed and proposed hiring a technical writer as one of the first non-engineering roles, possibly in Q3.

[Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) walked through the go-to-market landscape. He'd been running discovery calls with mid-market companies in logistics and fintech, and the recurring theme was that prospects don't fully understand deterministic infrastructure yet but get very excited once they see the demo. Miro floated the idea of a "zero-to-pipeline" interactive walkthrough that could replace the slide deck entirely. There was general enthusiasm for this.

[Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) covered the design and developer experience roadmap. Tal showed early wireframes for a dashboard that would give operators visibility into pipeline health — compression ratios, authentication status, throughput. The group debated whether to ship a CLI-first experience or invest in the dashboard early. Consensus landed on CLI-first with the dashboard as a fast follow, targeting mid-Q2 for an alpha.

The meeting closed with Seren outlining Q2 OKRs: land two design partners, ship the public SDK, and finalize the seed fundraising timeline. Tone throughout was energetic but grounded — a small team that knows exactly how much there is still to build.
timeline: - **2026-01-10** | Q1 OKRs finalized by [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss); five key results set across engineering, GTM, and developer experience.
- **2026-02-14** | [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) identifies nondeterminism bug in batching layer; debugging effort consumes ~3 weeks and delays SDK packaging.
- **2026-03-18** | [Miro Bergmann](people/miro-bergmann) completes first round of discovery calls — 11 conversations across logistics and fintech verticals.
- **2026-03-28** | [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) shares early dashboard wireframes with the team for async feedback ahead of all-hands.
- **2026-04-01** | Q1 all-hands held; 3 of 5 OKRs hit, Q2 priorities set around design partners, public SDK, and seed timeline.
- **2026-04-15** | CLI alpha shipped internally; [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) and [Tal Kimura](people/tal-kimura) begin dogfooding with synthetic workloads.
- **2026-06-01** | First design partner agreement signed following Miro's interactive walkthrough demo.
- **2026-07-01** | Q2 all-hands scheduled; expected topics include seed raise progress and public SDK launch readiness.

_facts.name: Anchor All-Hands Q1 2026
_facts.topic: Company all-hands — Q1 2026 review, OKRs, and roadmap
